reasoning: The Manifold market valued this question at 2% on Dec 11, with only 20 days remaining. Our search into the geopolitical developments of 2025 strongly validates this low probability. Despite initial attempts by the Trump administration to negotiate a nuclear deal in early 2025, talks collapsed by June following an Israeli strike and a broader '12-day war.' The US even conducted military strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities later in the year. In October 2025, Iran officially announced the end of the 2015 JCPOA agreement, and the UN resumed sanctions via the snapback mechanism. A December 9, 2025 INSS report confirmed that diplomatic negotiations are in a 'deadlock' and no concrete steps have been taken to resume them. Considering the severe deterioration in US-Iran relations, recent military hostilities, and the complete lack of ongoing diplomacy, it is extremely unlikely that a sudden mutual agreement will be announced in the final 10 days of the year. I align with the market consensus of 2%.
